中篇小說【珍妃玉如意】The Precious Consort's Jade Ruyi · A Historical Novella

歷史與命運，並不總是確定不變。歷史是被「人」記錄的，有時並非真實的全部，有時被人有意選擇、刪減、甚至竄改。因此，在文字記載的史實之外，是否還存在某些被故意刪減、隱瞞、甚至竄改的歷史？ 作者野山鹿鳴創作的中篇小說《珍妃玉如意》，就是在推論這樣一種可能性後，有感而發。 八國聯軍即將攻進北京之際，慈禧太后在倉惶逃離北京前，下令將光緒皇帝的寵妃珍妃投入井中。這一事件，已經是歷史定論。 然而，有時歷史也會開玩笑，珍妃是否真的死了？還是在關鍵時刻，被人救出，逃離宮廷，開始了她的另一段隱秘人生？ 本書故事即由此展開。
本系列文章目錄
魂去來兮The Soul Returns Home 譚宇熙·心痛絕望的派出所所長Tan Yuxi, the Heartbroken and Desperate Police Station Chief

The Precious Consort's Jade Ruyi · A Historical Novella中篇小說【珍妃玉如意】

History and fate are not always as fixed or as certain as they appear. History is recorded by human beings. What is preserved in the written record is not always the whole truth. At times, facts are deliberately selected, omitted, concealed, or even altered. Beyond the history that has come down to us, might there still exist another history—one that was intentionally erased, hidden, or rewritten? The Precious Consort’s Jade Ruyi, a novella by Yeshan Luming, was inspired by the exploration of just such a possibility. As the armies of the Eight-Nation Alliance were about to enter Beijing, Empress Dowager Cixi, preparing to flee the capital in panic, ordered that Emperor Guangxu’s beloved Precious Consort Zhen be thrown into a well. This event has long been accepted as an established historical fact. But history sometimes plays strange tricks. Did Consort Zhen truly die that day? Or was she rescued at the final moment, spirited out of the Forbidden City, and given the chance to begin another life—one hidden forever from the official chronicles? This is where the story begins.
Series Directory
The Soul Returns Home魂去來兮 Tan Yuxi, the Heartbroken and Desperate Police Station Chief譚宇熙·心痛絕望的派出所所長
